mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the past 6 months indicates rising inflation expectations, leading to higher nominal interest rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive despite CPI increases exceeding 2% over 6 months, or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4% indicates a tightening labor market, leading to higher wage pressures and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive despite UNRATE declining below 4%, or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in industrial production (INDPRO) over the past 3 months indicates a strong economic expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain negative despite INDPRO increases exceeding 5% over 3 months, or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in housing starts (HOUST) over the past 6 months indicates a strong housing market, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain negative despite HOUST increases exceeding 10% over 6 months, or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) over the past 3 months indicates a tightening monetary cycle, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive despite FEDFUNDS increases exceeding 1% over 3 months, or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in real GDP (GDPC1) over the past year indicates a strong economic expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days, but only if the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is below 5%.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain negative despite GDPC1 increases exceeding 5% over 1 year and UNRATE below 5%, or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) over the past 3 months indicates a strong labor market, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, but only if the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) is below 3%.
regime_hint: strong labor market with low inflation
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain negative despite PAYEMS increases exceeding 2% over 3 months and CPIAUCSL below 3%, or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
